claim_bullets.bullet_text: Functions with one or more free variables are called **closures** (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.44-48).
Pure functions, which have no free variables, are the easiest to understand and **may contain closures** (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.44-48).
A free variable is a name that is not bound within a function; it is resolved from a parent environment (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.44-48).

source_record_text: This section explains how nested functions in JavaScript capture free variables, defining closures and pure functions, and discusses environments and scope chains.
